subject: [PATCH v4 3/4] sunrpc: guarantee a thread per CPU-bearing node when auto-distributing

reviews.author: NeilBrown
reviews.summary: Reviewer NeilBrown noted that the condition in svc_set_num_threads() is unnecessary because svc_pool_map_init_pernode() ensures each node with a pool has at least one CPU, and suggested addressing the problem outside the loop by setting remain to serv->sv_nrpools when base is zero and nrservs is non-zero.
reviews.raw_body:
svc_pool_map_init_pernode() uses for_each_node_with_cpus() so we can be
certain that each node which has been allocated a pool will have at
least 1 cpu.  Thus that last condition isn't needed.

I would probably address the problem outside the loop with

if (base == 0 && nrservs != 0)
   /* We need at least one thread per pool for correct functionality */
   remain = serv->sv_nrpools;

or similar.  But your version works too and this isn't
performance-critical code.

Reviewed-by: NeilBrown <neil@brown.name>

Thanks,
NeilBrown

reviews.author: Jeff Layton (author)
reviews.summary: Author agrees to use NeilBrown's suggested modification, which guarantees a thread per CPU-bearing node when auto-distributing threads.
reviews.raw_body:
I like your version better. I'll adopt it.

Thanks!
-- 
Jeff Layton <jlayton@kernel.org>

2026-07-02.patch_summary: This patch ensures that each CPU-bearing node in a NUMA system has at least one thread when distributing RPC threads automatically. When the requested number of threads is less than the number of pools, some nodes' pools would be left empty under even distribution. The patch floors each pool's thread count to 1 for CPU-bearing nodes, preventing empty pools and potential service disruptions. This change only affects automatic thread distribution and does not impact explicit per-pool thread counts set by callers. Nodes without CPUs are unaffected.
